Read the text: 'The Amazon rainforest is the largest tropical rainforest in the world, covering an area of 5.5 million square kilometers. It is home to an incredible variety of plants and animals, many of which are not found anywhere else on Earth. The rainforest plays a crucial role in regulating the Earth's climate.' What is the main idea of the text?
options: The size of the Amazon rainforest.
The animals in the Amazon rainforest.
The importance and characteristics of the Amazon rainforest.
The climate of the Amazon rainforest.
questions.answer: The importance and characteristics of the Amazon rainforest.

questions.question: Read the text from Q1. The word 'variety' is closest in meaning to ____.
Read the text from Q1. What does 'crucial role' mean?
Read the text from Q1. According to the text, why is the Amazon rainforest important for our planet?
options: Because it has many unique animals.
Because it helps control the Earth's climate.
Because it is in South America.
questions.answer: Because it helps control the Earth's climate.

questions.question: Read the text from Q1. What can be inferred from the text?
options: All animals in the Amazon can be found in other places.
The Amazon rainforest is not important for the climate.
Protecting the Amazon rainforest is important for the planet.
The Amazon rainforest is getting smaller.
questions.answer: Protecting the Amazon rainforest is important for the planet.

questions.question: Read the text: 'Solar energy is energy from the sun. It is a renewable source of energy, which means it will not run out. People use solar panels to convert sunlight into electricity. This electricity can be used to power homes and businesses. Using solar energy is a clean way to produce power because it does not create pollution.' What is solar energy?
Read the text from Q6. What does 'renewable' mean?
Read the text from Q6. How do people capture solar energy?
Read the text from Q6. Why is solar energy considered 'clean'?
options: Because it is easy to find.
Because it comes from the sun.
Because it does not produce pollution.
questions.answer: Because it does not produce pollution.

questions.question: Read the text from Q6. The main purpose of the text is to ____.
options: persuade people to use solar energy.
explain what solar energy is and its benefits.
compare solar energy with other types of energy.
questions.answer: explain what solar energy is and its benefits.

questions.question: Read the text: 'The internet has changed our lives in many ways. We can use it to find information, communicate with friends and family, shop online, and entertain ourselves. However, spending too much time online can be harmful. It's important to have a balance between online and offline activities.' What is the text mainly about?
options: The pros and cons of the internet.
questions.answer: The pros and cons of the internet.

questions.question: Read the text from Q11. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a use of the internet?
Read the text from Q11. The word 'harmful' is the opposite of ____.
Read the text from Q11. What is the author's advice?
options: We should not use the internet.
We should spend all our time online.
We should balance online and offline time.
We should only use the internet for shopping.
questions.answer: We should balance online and offline time.

questions.question: Read the text from Q11. The word 'communicate' means ____.

Read the text: 'Recycling is the process of converting waste materials into new materials and objects. It is an important way to reduce pollution and conserve natural resources. Common recyclable materials include paper, glass, plastic, and metal. By recycling, we can help protect the environment for the future.' What is recycling?
Read the text from Q16. Which of the following is NOT a common recyclable material?
Read the text from Q16. The word 'conserve' means to ____.
Read the text from Q16. What is the main benefit of recycling mentioned in the text?
Read the text from Q16. The tone of the text is ____.

Read the text: 'The Great Wall of China is one of the most impressive man-made structures in the world. It was built over many centuries to protect China from invasions. The wall is more than 21,000 kilometers long and is a popular tourist attraction today.' Why was the Great Wall of China built?
options: As a place for emperors to live.

questions.question: Read the text from Q21. The word 'impressive' means ____.
Read the text from Q21. What is the Great Wall's length?

Read the text from Q21. What is the Great Wall today?
